idea: User's 'carousel': real traffic is bursty, so fixed query-tiling either waits to fill a tile (latency) or scans partial tiles (wasted bandwidth). Instead keep each worker continuously cycling the base; an arriving query attaches at the current cursor, rides one full revolution (sees all N docs), leaves with top-C. Each doc read is shared by everyone aboard = dynamic tiling with tile=in-flight count. Classic scan-sharing (Crescando/Blink). New bin src/bin/carousel.rs; self-contained Poisson-arrival benchmark.
note: Carousel (8 independent full-base carousels, seats=8) vs per-query (017 model), bursty Poisson load. Per-query: ~12ms p50 until ~500 QPS capacity, then LATENCY CLIFF (533ms@600, 2045ms@800 - queue explodes). Carousel: NO cliff, ~30-35ms p50 / ~60ms p99 FLAT across 400-800 QPS, and higher sustained capacity (scan-sharing). Trade: higher latency FLOOR at low load (77ms@100 vs 12ms). Idea CONFIRMED for bursty/overload (the intended scenario): bounded latency + graceful degradation + no cliff. Weakness = low-load floor (one full revolution); next: shard docs across workers to cut revolution ~8x (040).
